name: Lines of Torres Forts Trail
name_original: Forte do Moinho Branco a partir de Alhandra — percurso circular
description: This difficult 6.4-mile (10.3 km) jogging route from Alhandra offers historical exploration and panoramic views from Forte do Moinho Branco.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Alhandra are characterized by the town's position along the Tagus River, offering accessible pedestrian paths. The region features a developed riverside path that extends towards Vila Franca de Xira, providing varied running opportunities. This infrastructure supports outdoor activities, making the area suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Alhandra The most popular jogging route is Lines of Torres Forts Trail, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 17 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant elevation gain, offering a challenging run. Another top favourite among local runners is Parque da Praia dos Pescadores loop from Alverca, a moderate 6.9 miles (11.1 km) path. This route features minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for a steady pace through park and riverside scenery. Local runners also love the Running loop from Alverca, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail leading through local areas, often completed in about 1 hour 7 minutes. This moderate loop provides a consistent running experience with gentle gradients. Jogging around Alhandra is defined by riverside paths, pedestrian infrastructure, and views of the Tagus River. The network of running routes offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult runs, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Alhandra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Alhandra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Alhandra?
A: Alhandra offers a wide selection of running opportunities, with over 35 routes available for you to explore. These include a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging paths, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in Alhandra?
A: Yes, Alhandra has several easy running routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. While the majority are moderate, there is at least one designated easy route, and many of the moderate paths feature gentle gradients, especially along the riverside.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails around Alhandra?
A: The jogging trails in Alhandra are primarily characterized by riverside paths and pedestrian infrastructure along the Tagus River. You can expect mostly flat or gently undulating terrain, with some routes offering more significant elevation changes for a challenging run, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381191366/lines-of-torres-forts-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lines of Torres Forts Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options in Alhandra?
A: For those seeking longer runs, Alhandra offers several routes that extend beyond 10 kilometers. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32200773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tanque de Maré – Ponto de Observação e Estadia loop from Alverca&lt;/a&gt; is over 22 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32209440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tanque de Maré loop from Sobralinho&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 20 km, providing ample distance for endurance training.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Alhandra?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Alhandra are designed as loops, offering convenient circular paths. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19409824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque da Praia dos Pescadores loop from Alverca&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32169719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Alverca&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What are the best times of year to go running in Alhandra?
A: Alhandra's mild climate makes it suitable for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures. Summer mornings and evenings are also popular to avoid the midday heat, while winter runs can be refreshing, especially along the riverside paths.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running trails in Alhandra?
A: The accessible pedestrian paths along the Tagus River, including the Alhandra riverside path, are generally suitable for families. These flatter sections provide a safe and enjoyable environment for a family jog or walk, often with scenic views and outdoor gym equipment.

Q: What points of interest can I see while running in Alhandra?
A: While jogging in Alhandra, you can enjoy views of the Tagus River and its estuary. Nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6301486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Constantino Palha Municipal Garden&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6301470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cais da Jorna and Fishermen's Monument&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2862008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alhandra Picnic Park&lt;/a&gt;, which are great spots to pause and take in the local scenery.

Q: Is public transport or parking available near the running routes in Alhandra?
A: Alhandra is well-connected, and the presence of a developed riverside path suggests good accessibility. While specific parking details for each route vary, the town's infrastructure generally supports access by both public transport and car, with parking options likely available near the main pedestrian areas.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging experience in Alhandra?
A: The running routes in Alhandra are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an impressive average rating of 4.8 stars from over 5 reviews. Runners often praise the scenic riverside paths, the variety of terrain, and the overall pleasant environment for outdoor exercise.

Q: Are there any amenities like cafes or rest stops along the running routes?
A: The Alhandra riverside path and its proximity to the town mean you'll likely find amenities such as cafes and rest stops within easy reach. The promenade itself enhances the local area, suggesting opportunities for refreshments before or after your run.
tips: Founded in 1558 by Cardinal D. Henrique, the main church was a majestic temple for the time. After a fire, it was completely rebuilt with simple, unadorned architecture. Its interior, however, houses a valuable collection of sacred art from the 17th and 18th centuries, including pieces from the original church and other churches in Lisbon.

Designed by Lieutenant Colonel Joaquim da Costa Cascaes and approved in 1874, the monument commemorates the Anglo-Portuguese victory over Napoleon and the construction of the Lines of Torres Vedras. Completed in 1883, it features a statue of Hercules, the work of the sculptor Simões de Almeida, with classical attributes.

The Lines of Torres Vedras are 152 defensive fortifications, built between 1809 and 1812 near Lisbon, which prevented the French advance in the Peninsular War.
The Forte do Moinho Branco (or Sinais) is one of these buildings.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Forte do Moinho Branco circular route for jogging?
faqs.answer: This route is considered a difficult 'Hard run' for jogging, requiring 'Very good fitness'. While it features mostly accessible paths, some sections demand sure-footedness due to varied terrain. For comparison, it is rated as a 'Moderate hike' suitable for all skill levels with good fitness.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, spanning approximately 10.3 kilometers with significant elevation changes. You can expect dirt paths, gravel, and potentially some rocky or root-filled sections, particularly on inclines and declines. Sure-footedness is required in certain areas.

faqs.question: What historical sites or viewpoints can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and historical significance. You'll pass by the Parish Church of Saint John the Baptist and the Hercules Monument and Viewpoint. The primary highlight is the Forte do Moinho Branco itself, a historical military fortification offering spectacular panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Forte do Moinho Branco circular route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Alhandra. While specific parking details aren't provided, Alhandra is the starting point, suggesting parking options would be available within the town or near the trailhead. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: How can I get to the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: Alhandra is accessible by public transport. You can typically reach Alhandra from Lisbon via train or bus services. Once in Alhandra, the trailhead for the circular route should be within walking distance from the main transport hubs.

faqs.question: Is the Forte do Moinho Branco circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, allowing you to bring your canine companion. However, given the varied terrain and the need for sure-footedness, ensure your dog is fit for a challenging 10km run with elevation changes. Always keep your dog on a leash and clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog the Forte do Moinho Branco circular route. The historical sites along the way, including the fort, are generally open for public access.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this route is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these seasons, the weather is generally milder, making the significant elevation changes more manageable and the panoramic views more enjoyable without the intense heat of summer.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Caminho Português Central, Caminhos de Fátima, Rua Marquês da Subserra, Calçada da Igreja, Trilho do Miratejo, Estrada Militar do Casal de Vila Quente, Rua da Escola Sousa Martins, Travessa da Escusa, Estrada do Miradouro, Beco das Parreirinhas, Trilho dos Pinheiros, Travessa das Parreirinhas, Beco do Castelo, Beco da Euterpe, Travessa Dona Amália, Beco do Cais, Travessa da Manutenção Militar, and Trilho Adeformoso XC.

faqs.question: What should I bring for jogging this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring appropriate jogging shoes with good grip, sufficient water, and snacks. A small first-aid kit, a fully charged phone, and sun protection are also advisable. Layers of clothing are useful for adapting to changing weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants near the trailhead in Alhandra?
faqs.answer: Yes, Alhandra is a town, so you can expect to find various amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and possibly accommodation options. These can be useful for pre- or post-run refreshments and meals.

waypoints.name: Parish Church of Saint John the Baptist
